mnemonics.title: OSI LAYERS
mnemonics.content: PLEASE DO NOT THROW SAUSAGE PIZZA AWAY - Physical, Data Link, Network, Transport, Session, Presentation, Application, (Note: Some use 'All' instead of Away)

mnemonics.title: APPLICATION LAYER PROTOCOLS
mnemonics.content: HASE - HTTP, FTP, SMTP, DNS / DHCP / SNMP (H-A-S-E: How Applications Send Email)

mnemonics.title: WELL-KNOWN PORTS
mnemonics.content: FAST 21-25: FTP(21), SSH(22), Telnet(23), SMTP(25) - Remember as 'Fast twenty-one through twenty-five'

acronyms.usage: Reference model for network communication with 7 layers
Set of protocols enabling software applications to communicate
Translates domain names to IP addresses
Automatically assigns IP addresses to network devices
Protocol for web communication between clients and servers
Protocol for transferring files between client and server
Protocol for sending emails between mail servers
Connectionless, unreliable transport protocol with low overhead
Network model where nodes share resources directly with each other

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a building with 7 floors - Application is the TOP floor (where users interact), Physical is the BASEMENT (where data moves physically). Information flows DOWN from Application to Physical when sending, and UP when receiving.
Draw a restaurant: The SERVER is like the kitchen (prepares and serves food), The CLIENT is like the customer (places orders and receives food). Waiter acts as the network connection.
Think of sockets as phone extensions in an office. The server is like the main office number (port), clients call this number. Once connected, they have their own private line (established socket).

Imagine wrapping a gift: Application data is the gift, Transport adds bubble wrap (TCP/UDP header), Network adds a shipping box (IP header), Data Link adds the delivery truck (frame), Physical is the road.

analogies.analogy: Like a customer service representative in a company - they interact directly with customers (users) to fulfill requests, but don't handle the internal logistics (which lower layers handle).
Like apartment numbers in a large building. The IP address is the building address, and port numbers are the specific apartment numbers where different services live.

analogies.anal analogy: Like a customer filling out a form at a bank. The request form (HTTP request) tells the bank what you want, the bank processes it, and gives you a response (HTTP response) with the result.

analogies.analogy: Like a telephone system - one person sets up a phone to receive calls (server socket), another dials the number to connect (client socket), once connected they can talk (data transfer).
Like a phone book - you look up a name (domain name like google.com) to find the phone number (IP address) to make a call.
